breaks: The proposed rule that authenticated "INTERACTIVE elements are outside" identical-page comparison fails after completion, when REQ-015 makes the thread read-only while its history remains readable; thread history is viewer-specific read content, not merely an interactive control.

The proposed "two viewer-specific elements" count is already false. REQ-010 restricts reference-file downloads and gives only the NGO add/remove rights; REQ-015 gives only the NGO task-anchored-comment controls; and project-page fuel/Lovable top-up, workspace-access, and blocker actions are also role-gated. The gauges and needs-action facts may be public, but their controls are not.

Leaving "REQ-015 unchanged" leaves the decisive rule undefined. "A project-page comment thread ... (NGO admins, the assigned volunteer, and the platform admin only when escalated)" plus "membership is implicit from project roles" can mean participant-only read/post, but it also permits the simpler public-read/participant-post interpretation; AT-015.01 currently invents restricted reading.

The carve-out cannot imply that all thread-adjacent information is private: REQ-010 already makes the task tree, activity feed, blocker signal, and lifetime Q&A log public. System events, accepted scope additions, and clarification records can therefore expose derived facts publicly even if raw thread history is restricted.

The authoritative PRD's "one viewer-specific element" wording originates in REQ-010, not REQ-033 or the Platform Promise. REQ-033 and the Promise need no semantic change, but the extracted REQ-010, AT-010.01, and architecture note repeating "one" must be synchronized.
tensions.amended_resolution: Replace numerical exception-counting with a two-layer contract: an identical public project-status projection and separately role-gated communications and controls governed by their owning requirements. The recommended founder ruling is that raw REQ-015 thread history and posting are both limited to current participants—the project's NGO account, currently assigned volunteer, and platform admin only while escalated—with posting frozen at terminal states; public-read/participant-post is otherwise a defensible alternative that the present PRD does not exclude. Expressly public projections such as tasks, activity, blocker severity/count, Q&A records, file metadata, funding status, cadence, and repo signals remain public even when derived from restricted interactions. AT-010 should compare only that public projection and separately test each owning requirement's access rules; AT-015 should test the founder-selected read rule through escalation, release/reassignment, and terminal states.
tensions.minimal_prd_change: In REQ-010, replace the opening equality sentence and the "one viewer-specific element" bullet with: "One public page per project, whose public project-status projection is identical for every viewer. That projection comprises the expressly public surfaces below, including project identity, the full task tree, activity, blocker signal, Q&A log, reference-file metadata, fuel/Lovable status, cadence, and repo-derived signals. Role-gated communication content and action controls are outside this equality rule and follow their owning requirements; the NGO project assistant follows REQ-033." In REQ-015, add after its opening sentence: "Only current thread participants may read its history or post: the project's NGO account, the currently assigned volunteer, and the platform admin only while escalated; role changes update access automatically, and after completion or cancellation authorized readers retain history access but posting is disabled."

overall: T7 needs a founder ruling because REQ-015 does not unambiguously choose participant-only reading over public reading with participant-only posting, and that choice changes the product's privacy boundary rather than merely correcting prose.
